timeline.label: FY2025 10-K: declared distributions $2.775346/unit on 48.26M units; revenues $152.8M
2025 unit-funded mineral acquisitions: $3.888M royalty consideration received
2024 Colorado and Permian mineral exchanges: $14.725M royalty consideration; ~14,225 net mineral acres in Sep 2024 deal
Carrollton Mineral Partners amended SC 13D/A (activist/long context)
Carrollton Mineral Partners initial SC 13D filing
Prior mineral acquisition cycle: $2.284M royalty consideration (2023)
Partnership maintains 100% available-cash distribution policy with minimal balance-sheet debt
Long-standing institutional holder SC 13G/A on file (governance context)

industry.structure: U.S. oil and gas royalty and mineral MLPs compete with trusts, public E&Ps, and private mineral buyers for acreage; Dorchester is capital-light with no operating employees and thousands of small properties.
industry.share_shift: Consolidators and private mineral funds acquire Permian and Rockies acreage; Dorchester grows via unit-for-mineral exchanges rather than operating capex.
industry.trend: Commodity prices and operator activity drive quarterly distributions; 2024-2025 acquisitions add non-producing inventory that may convert to cash over time.
